summary: Parse `[role.<name>]` tables in temper.toml into a typed role model on AuthorLayer: artifact kind, contract ref, match selector, required, verified_by — parse-only foundation.

edit.description: Add a typed `Role` model and parse a top-level `[role.<name>]` table on `AuthorLayer`: artifact kind, contract reference (a template path string or an inline `[[clause]]` array reusing `contract::parse_clauses`), a decidable `match` selector (closed set: name-glob or an explicit `role:`/opt-in marker — pattern stored verbatim, not evaluated), an optional `required` flag (absent ⇒ false), and an optional `verified_by` string. A malformed role table is a load error (new `ComposeError` arms), mirroring the existing `[kind.<k>]` parse. Unit tests for each parse path and the malformed-role error; the existing kind-layer tests are untouched.

tests.asserts: a full `[role.<name>]` table parses into a typed `Role`; an inline-`[[clause]]` contract and a path-string contract both parse; an absent `required` defaults false; a malformed role is a load error; a temper.toml with only `[kind.*]` carries an empty roster.

acceptance: `AuthorLayer` carries a parsed role roster: `[role.<name>]` tables load into typed `Role` values (artifact kind, contract ref, match selector, required, verified_by); a malformed role is a load error; existing kind-layer behaviour is unchanged. `cargo test` + `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Scope is parse only — no evaluation. Selection + `required` single-filler conformance, and admissibility (`match` resolves, `verified_by` resolves per 10-contracts) are separate follow-on entries. `match` is the closed decidable selector; the glob is stored verbatim, not matched, so no new crate. Absent `required` defaults false — temper never fabricates a gate the author didn't declare (00-intent law 4).
